2604.10718.title: SciPredict: Can LLMs Predict the Outcomes of Scientific Experiments in Natural Sciences?
2604.10718.paper_summary: Accelerating scientific discovery requires the identification of which experiments would yield the best outcomes before committing resources to costly physical validation. While existing benchmarks evaluate LLMs on scientific knowledge and reasoning, their ability to predict experimental outcomes - a task where AI could significantly exceed human capabilities - remains largely underexplored. We introduce SciPredict, a benchmark comprising 405 tasks derived from recent empirical studies in 33 specialized sub-fields of physics, biology, and chemistry. SciPredict addresses two critical questions: (a) can LLMs predict the outcome of scientific experiments with sufficient accuracy? and (b) can such predictions be reliably used in the scientific research process? Evaluations reveal fundamental limitations on both fronts. Model accuracies are 14-26% and human expert performance is $\approx$20%. Although some frontier models exceed human performance model accuracy is still far below what would enable reliable experimental guidance. Even within the limited performance, models fail to distinguish reliable predictions from unreliable ones, achieving only $\approx$20% accuracy regardless of their confidence or whether they judge outcomes as predictable without physical experimentation. Human experts, in contrast, demonstrate strong calibration: their accuracy increases from $\approx$5% to $\approx$80% as they deem outcomes more predictable without conducting the experiment. SciPredict establishes a rigorous framework demonstrating that superhuman performance in experimental science requires not just better predictions, but better awareness of prediction reliability. For reproducibility all our data and code are provided at https://github.com/scaleapi/scipredict
2604.10718.paper_summary_zh: 加速科學發現需要在投入資源進行昂貴的實體驗證之前，確定哪些實驗會產生最佳結果。雖然現有的基準評估 LLM 在科學知識和推理方面的表現，但它們預測實驗結果的能力——這是一個 AI 可以顯著超越人類能力的任務——仍然在很大程度上未被探索。我們推出了 SciPredict，這是一個基準，包含來自 33 個專門子領域的 405 個任務，這些子領域包括物理學、生物學和化學，均源自近期的實證研究。SciPredict 解決了兩個關鍵問題：（a）LLM 能否以足夠的準確性預測科學實驗的結果？以及（b）這些預測能否在科學研究過程中可靠地使用？評估顯示在這兩方面都存在根本的限制。模型的準確率為 14-26%，而人類專家的表現約為 20%。儘管一些前沿模型超越了人類表現，但模型的準確率仍遠低於能夠提供可靠實驗指導的水平。即使在有限的表現中，模型也無法區分可靠的預測和不可靠的預測，無論其信心如何，或是是否認為結果在沒有實體實驗的情況下是可預測的，其準確率僅約為 20%。相比之下，人類專家顯示出強大的校準能力：當他們認為結果在不進行實驗的情況下更可預測時，他們的準確率從約 5% 增加到約 80%。SciPredict 建立了一個嚴謹的框架，表明在實驗科學中超越人類的表現不僅需要更好的預測，還需要對預測可靠性的更好認識。為了可重複性，我們的所有數據和代碼均提供於 https://github.com/scaleapi/scipredict
2604.10718.author: Udari Madhushani Sehwag et.al.
2604.10718.authors: Udari Madhushani Sehwag, Elaine Lau, Haniyeh Ehsani Oskouie, Shayan Shabihi, Erich Liang, Andrea Toledo, Guillermo Mangialardi, Sergio Fonrouge, Ed-Yeremai Hernandez Cardona, Paula Vergara, Utkarsh Tyagi, Chen Bo Calvin Zhang, Pavi Bhatter, Nicholas Johnson, Furong Huang, Ernesto Gabriel Hernandez Montoya, Bing Liu
